text.title: mcp-servers Failure Modes
text.summary: Migrated antipatterns for mcp-servers as failure modes. Add detector + repair clauses where missing.

failure-mode.description: Using `npx -y` without pinning a version. This pulls the latest version on each Claude Code startup, causing silent breaking changes in upstream packages.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Version unpinning introduces non-deterministic behavior. Always specify a version: `npx -y @anthropic/github-mcp@1.2.3`.

failure-mode.description: MCP server crashes are not surfaced clearly to the agent — they produce "tool unavailable" errors instead of actionable error messages.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Always verify server availability with `claude mcp list` before expecting agents to use a server.

failure-mode.description: Storing tokens in plaintext in settings.json. `claude mcp add` writes credentials to `~/.claude/settings.json` without encryption.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Use 1Password integration or a secrets manager; inject tokens via environment variables, never hardcode.

failure-mode.description: Scope servers to `user` scope for personal auth. Project scope only for team-shared, non-secret config.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Project-scoped secrets would be committed to version control.

failure-mode.description: Agents cannot dynamically add MCP servers mid-session — all servers must be registered before Claude Code starts.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Use a setup agent or script (`mcp-setup.sh`) that runs once to register all required servers.
